info.name: Deal Registration APIs
info.description: APIs for creating, updating, submitting, searching, and tracking partner deal and service registrations, as well as listing partner accounts and requesting extensions.

item.name: [Step 2]: List partner accounts accessible to the caller

request.description:
**List Partner Accounts**.
Returns the list of partner accounts that the authenticated caller can act on, including the account name, roles, and primary account indicator. This is typically used by clients to populate a partner account selector before calling registration APIs with `X-Partner-Account-Id`.

response.name: Partner accounts retrieved successfully.

header.description: Optional partner account identifier. Included for consistency with other endpoints but not required to list the caller's accessible accounts.

description.content: A unique identifier assigned to each request that can be used to trace a specific request.

response.name: Missing or invalid authentication token.

header.description: Optional partner account identifier. Included for consistency with other endpoints but not required to list the caller's accessible accounts.

description.content: A unique identifier assigned to each request that can be used to trace a specific request.

response.name: Caller lacks permission to list partner accounts.

header.description: Optional partner account identifier. Included for consistency with other endpoints but not required to list the caller's accessible accounts.

description.content: A unique identifier assigned to each request that can be used to trace a specific request.

response.name: Internal server error while listing partner accounts.

header.description: Optional partner account identifier. Included for consistency with other endpoints but not required to list the caller's accessible accounts.

description.content: A unique identifier assigned to each request that can be used to trace a specific request.

item.name: [Step 3]: Create a new draft deal registration

header.description: (Required) Unique identifier of the partner account on whose behalf the registration is being created. The caller must have permission to act on this partner.

body.raw:
{
  "dealName": "Test Deal",
  "programType": "DEAL_REGISTRATION",
  "partnerSubmittedOpportunityNumber": "CRM-1234",
  "products": [
    "JIRA",
    "CONFLUENCE"
  ],
  "customerHasCloudSite": "Yes",
  "cloudSite": "mytestsite.com",
  "notes": "This is a test Deal.",
  "detailedDescription": "This is a test Deal.",
  "platform": "CLOUD",
  "estimatedRevenue": 100000,
  "customerContactEmailId": "ddomingo2@20test.com",
  "customerDomain": "20test.com",
  "partnerSalesRepresentativeEmailId": "aleonhardt+3@usersinbuckets.com",
  "estimatedOpportunityCloseDate": "2026-03-31",
  "comments": "Please review",
  "budgetAllocated": "YES",
  "customerKeyDecisionMakersEngagedInProcess": true,
  "crucialEvent": "New Licence  Purchase",
  "rfpRelated": true,
  "rfpRelatedTo": "GENERAL_SOLUTION_PROPOSAL",
  "salesType": "NEW_PRODUCT",
  "eccListedAboveIsKeyDecisionMaker": true
  
}

request.description: **Create Deal Registration Draft**. Creates a new **draft** deal registration for an existing partner account. The draft can later be updated and submitted asynchronously for processing. This API supports only existing customers and existing customer contacts; it does **not** create new customer or contact records.

response.name: Draft deal registration created successfully.

header.description: (Required) Unique identifier of the partner account on whose behalf the registration is being created. The caller must have permission to act on this partner.

description.content: A unique identifier assigned to each request that can be used to trace a specific request.

item.name: [Step 3]: Create a new draft service registration

header.description: (Required) Unique identifier of the partner account on whose behalf the registration is being created. The caller must have permission to act on this partner.

body.raw:
{
  "dealName": "Test Service Registration",
  "programType": "SERVICE_REGISTRATION",
  "partnerSubmittedOpportunityNumber": "CRM-5678",
  "products": [
    "BITBUCKET",
    "COMPASS"
  ],
  "customerHasCloudSite": "Yes",
  "cloudSite": "mytestsite.com",
  "notes": "My Sample Service Registration",
  "detailedDescription": "My Sample Service Registration Detailed notes",
  "platform": "CLOUD",
  "estimatedRevenue": 200002,
  "customerContactEmailId": "test@20test.com",
  "customerDomain": "20test.com",
  "partnerSalesRepresentativeEmailId": "aleonhardt+4@usersinbuckets.com",
  "estimatedOpportunityCloseDate": "2026-03-31",
  "comments": "Need review",
  "solution": "CLOUD_MIGRATION",
  "regions": [
    "AMER",
    "EMEA"
  ],
  "departments": [
    "HR",
    "IT"
  ],
  "serviceType": [
    "PROJECT_SUPPORT_AND_MAINTENANCE_SUPPORT_SERVICES",
    "PARTNER_SOLUTION_CONSULTING_ARCHITECTURE"
  ],
  "serviceEngagementStage": "DELIVERY_NOT_STARTED",

  "serviceEndDate": "2026-03-15",
  "deliveryConsultant": "test@20test.com",
  "deliveryConsultantEmail": "test@20test.com",
  "userCount": 100,
  "entityType": "ORDER_NUMBER",
  "entityIds": [
    "AT-187916420"
  ]
}

item.name: [Step 4]: Search registrations for a partner account

header.description: (Required) Unique identifier of the partner account whose registrations are being searched. The caller must have permission to act on this partner.

query.description:
Optional comma-separated list of registration statuses to filter by. If omitted, results include registrations of all statuses visible to the partner.
Possible values include: `DRAFT`, `USE_CHILD_REGISTRATIONS`, `SUBMITTED`, `APPROVED`, `REJECTED`, `CLOSED_WON`, `CLOSED_LOST`, `CLOSED`, `RETURNED`, `FIELD_APPROVED`, `ON_HOLD`, `EXPIRED`, `PENDING`, `UNDER_REVIEW`.
Filter by specific registration ID (for example, `D-xxxx` or `CD-xxxx`).
Filter by parent registration ID to retrieve child registrations (for example, `D-xxxx`).

Optional comma-separated list of program types to filter by.
Supported values: * `DEAL_REGISTRATION` * `SERVICE_REGISTRATION`

Filter by registration hierarchy type.
* `PARENT` – top-level registrations. * `CHILD` – registrations that have a parentRegistrationId.
Start of creation time window (inclusive), in ISO 8601 date-time format (for example, `2024-01-01T00:00:00Z`).
End of creation time window (inclusive), in ISO 8601 date-time format (for example, `2024-12-31T23:59:59Z`).
Maximum number of registrations to return in a single page (page size). Default is 10; maximum is 100.
Opaque pagination token returned by a previous search response. Use this to fetch the next page of results.

Field to sort results by. Defaults to `CREATED_AT` when omitted.
Supported values: * `CREATED_AT` * `LAST_MODIFIED_AT` * `REGISTRATION_ID` * `STATUS` * `PROGRAM_TYPE`
Sort direction for the specified sort field. Defaults to `DESC` (newest first).

request.description:
**Search Registrations**.

 Searches deal and service registrations for a given partner account, with
 support for filtering by status, program type, registration identifiers,
 date ranges, and sorting with cursor-based pagination. Returns both draft
 and submitted registrations unless further filtered by status.

response.name: Search results returned successfully.

header.description: (Required) Unique identifier of the partner account whose registrations are being searched. The caller must have permission to act on this partner.

Possible values include: `DRAFT`, `USE_CHILD_REGISTRATIONS`, `SUBMITTED`, `APPROVED`, `REJECTED`, `CLOSED_WON`, `CLOSED_LOST`, `CLOSED`, `RETURNED`, `FIELD_APPROVED`, `ON_HOLD`, `EXPIRED`, `PENDING`, `UNDER_REVIEW`.
Filter by specific registration ID (for example, `D-xxxx` or `CD-xxxx`).
Filter by parent registration ID to retrieve child registrations (for example, `D-xxxx`).

Optional comma-separated list of program types to filter by.
Supported values: * `DEAL_REGISTRATION` * `SERVICE_REGISTRATION`

Filter by registration hierarchy type.
* `PARENT` – top-level registrations. * `CHILD` – registrations that have a parentRegistrationId.
Start of creation time window (inclusive), in ISO 8601 date-time format (for example, `2024-01-01T00:00:00Z`).
End of creation time window (inclusive), in ISO 8601 date-time format (for example, `2024-12-31T23:59:59Z`).

Supported values: * `CREATED_AT` * `LAST_MODIFIED_AT` * `REGISTRATION_ID` * `STATUS` * `PROGRAM_TYPE`
Sort direction for the specified sort field. Defaults to `DESC` (newest first).

request.description:
Business capability: **Update Deal Registration**. Partially updates fields on an existing deal registration (draft or submitted, depending on business rules). Only fields included in the request body are updated.
Business rules: * Draft registrations: all fields are editable. * Submitted registrations: only certain fields are editable (depends on approval status). * Approved / expired registrations: immutable and cannot be updated.

response.name: Deal registration updated successfully; no response body is returned.

header.description: (Required) Unique identifier of the partner account used to enforce visibility and access control for the registration being updated.

request.description:
**Submit Deal Registration (Async)**.
Queues a deal registration for asynchronous submission. This operation:
* Performs basic synchronous validations (auth, existence, partner visibility). * Persists an `async_submission_request` job with status `IN_PROGRESS`. * Enqueues the job for background processing. * Returns immediately with a `jobId` and `IN_PROGRESS` status.
The client must poll the job status endpoint to track progress until the job reaches a terminal state (`COMPLETED`, `FAILED`, `VALIDATION_ERROR`).

response.name: Submission queued for asynchronous processing.

header.description: (Required) Unique identifier of the partner account used to enforce visibility and access control for the registration being submitted.
Optional idempotency key used to avoid creating duplicate async jobs when the client retries the same submission request.

description.content: A unique identifier assigned to each request that can be used to trace a specific request.

item.name: [Step 9]: Get the status of an async submission job

header.description: (Required) Unique identifier of the partner account used to enforce visibility and access control for the async job and associated registration.

request.description:
**Track Registration Submission Job**.
Returns the current status and timestamps for an asynchronous registration submission job created by a submit endpoint. Clients can poll this endpoint until the job reaches a terminal state (`COMPLETED`, `FAILED`, `VALIDATION_ERROR`).

response.name: Job status retrieved successfully.

header.description: (Required) Unique identifier of the partner account used to enforce visibility and access control for the async job and associated registration.
